headlines.title: New attacks by Iran and U.S. strain an already shaky ceasefire
headlines.description: An Iran drone attack on Kuwait’s main airport killed one and injured dozens more. Still, late today President Trump dismissed the latest violence and said a longer term peace deal could be reached this weekend. NBC News’ Chief Foreign Correspondent Richard Engel reports from the region.

headlines.title: New video shows apparent Ukrainian attacks on Russian targets in St. Petersburg
headlines.description: New video shows apparent Ukrainian attacks on Russian targets in St. Petersburg during a major economic forum. NBC News' Keir Simmons reports.

headlines.title: Submit your questions for NBC News’ Keir Simmons
headlines.description: NBC News’ chief international correspondent will be in St. Petersburg, Russia, for a live Q&A. Submit your questions here.

headlines.title: DHS watchdog finds use-of-force issues and safety and sanitation concerns at Louisiana ICE center
headlines.description: A DHS watchdog report revealed that staff members at Winn Correctional Center used a prohibited chokehold to “gain control” of a person being held there and stabbed another in the hand with a pen when an officer could not close the door to a housing unit.

headlines.title: Steve Kornacki breaks down California's ‘cliffhanger’ and the first Trump-backed candidate loss
headlines.description: NBC News Chief Data Analyst Steve Kornacki joins Meet the Press NOW to break down the results of primary races across the nation and what Californians can expect on the ballot in the general election come November. KNBC-TV Political Reporter Conan Nolan analyzes the Los Angeles mayoral race.

headlines.title: In Iowa primary for governor, Trump’s pick concedes to MAHA-backed candidate
headlines.description: Rep. Randy Feenstra conceded to businessman Zach Lahn in Iowa’s Republican primary for governor as party voters rejected President Donald Trump’s late endorsement in the race.

headlines.title: Ohio State University reaches $100 million settlement in sex abuse lawsuits
headlines.description: All but one of the 280 former students who sued the school through five lawsuits for failing to protect them from a man accused of being a sexual predator have signed on to the agreement.

headlines.title: Student who called out CBS at News Emmys says Scott Pelley did the ‘right thing’
headlines.description: Moments before Santiago Campos took the stage at the News & Documentary Emmy Awards in New York City to accept a CBS News-funded scholarship in front of some of the most powerful people in TV news, veteran “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley took the time to offer the high school senior some advice.

headlines.title: New intel chief is a partisan warrior who has the president’s ear, sources say
headlines.description: Former officers worry Bill Pulte could use intelligence to target political opponents or justify federal oversight of elections.

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

headlines.title: Trump says he’s not sure if ‘anti-weaponization’ fund is really dead: ‘I’d have to ask the lawyers’
headlines.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said Tuesday the Justice Department was “not moving forward” with the $1.8 billion fund.

headlines.title: New intel chief is a partisan warrior who has the president’s ear, sources say
headlines.description: Former officers worry Bill Pulte could use intelligence to target political opponents or justify federal oversight of elections.
